architecturePolicy.coordinationPlacement: Outer application workflow assembled by Bootstrap and invoked by the existing Workforce HTTP compatibility adapter
architecturePolicy.coordinationAuthority: None; it coordinates provider-owned commands/queries and maps the frozen HTTP result but owns no business data
architecturePolicy.dependencyRule: The coordinator may call public application contracts. Bounded contexts may not import another context's repository, infrastructure, private domain, database model, or table.
architecturePolicy.cycleRule: Academic Groups, Scheduling, Lesson Delivery, and other consumers continue to depend on Workforce Teacher-reference contracts. Reverse reads needed by Workforce HTTP compatibility are performed by the outer coordinator, not by Workforce Domain/Application importing those consumers.
architecturePolicy.transactionRule: No target local transaction may write data owned by more than one bounded context. Exact ordering, compensation, retry, and idempotency are deferred to WF-PRE-11.
architecturePolicy.eventRule: WF-PRE-07 approves synchronous first-extraction seams only. No integration event is approved; WF-PRE-12 may supersede a seam with a versioned committed fact.
architecturePolicy.legacyRule: Current cross-table joins and writes remain frozen legacy debt. They are not target precedent or a temporary module exception.

contexts.name: Workforce
owns: Teacher Working Hour identity and policy

seams.direction: coordinator -> Workforce public command; coordinator -> Identity & Access public command

authority.identity-access: User, credential hash, Teacher role, access status, Branch grant, and sessions

seams.interaction: Create/update/archive is one compatibility workflow composed from provider-owned outcomes. Identity receives an opaque tenant-scoped Teacher reference and the minimum portal intent; it never persists Teacher business fields.
seams.failurePolicy: A required provider failure prevents a success response. No provider error may be translated into success or a partial DTO.
seams.consistencyBoundary: Each provider changes only its owned state. Cross-provider ordering, compensation, retry, and reconciliation remain blocking under WF-PRE-11.
forbidden: Workforce writes users, roles, user_roles, user_branch_access, or sessions
Identity writes teachers or teacher_working_hours
Either context imports the other's repository/infrastructure
seams.transition: Replace the shared SQLite transaction only after WF-PRE-09/10 contracts and WF-PRE-11 consistency policy are approved.

seams.capability: Teacher credential reset and session invalidation
seams.direction: coordinator -> Workforce Teacher-reference query; coordinator -> Identity & Access credential-reset command

authority.identity-access: Portal linkage, password validation/hashing, credential replacement, and session invalidation

seams.interaction: The compatibility route remains under the Workforce HTTP surface, but credential semantics and execution are delegated to Identity. The coordinator preserves the frozen 404/422/200 mapping.
seams.failurePolicy: Identity failure prevents success acknowledgement. Credential or session details are never returned to Workforce.
seams.consistencyBoundary: Credential replacement and invalidation of all sessions are one Identity-owned consistency requirement. Its exact unit-of-work and retry behavior are decided by WF-PRE-11.
forbidden: Workforce hashes or stores passwords
Workforce deletes sessions
Identity reads Teacher tables directly
seams.transition: The route adapter maps the Identity outcome to the frozen HTTP envelope; target Identity contract details remain WF-PRE-09/10.

seams.direction: coordinator -> Organization & Branches public reference query

authority.organization-branches: Branch existence, tenant ownership, active validity, and main/default selection
authority.workforce: Stores only the resolved BranchId reference on owned records

seams.interaction: Explicit Branch references are resolved for the active tenant; omitted references request the Organization-owned main/default Branch. Workforce does not infer defaults from Branch tables.
seams.failurePolicy: Invalid explicit reference fails closed with the compatibility-mapped validation result; provider unavailability prevents mutation.
seams.consistencyBoundary: Branch resolution is a precondition snapshot. Later Branch lifecycle changes do not transfer Branch ownership to Workforce.
forbidden: Workforce queries branches or selects a main Branch directly
Workforce creates or updates Branch state
Identity Branch grants define Branch business validity
seams.transition: WF-SEAM-RISK-01 blocks target Working Hour parity until the current unvalidated explicit branchId behavior receives governed compatibility treatment.

seams.direction: coordinator -> Academic Groups public blocker query

authority.academic-groups: Whether the Teacher has any tenant-active Group assignment that blocks archive
authority.workforce: Teacher archive decision after all external preconditions pass

seams.interaction: The provider returns a semantic blocker decision, not mutable Group rows or repository access.
seams.failurePolicy: Unknown or unavailable blocker state fails closed; Teacher is not archived.
seams.consistencyBoundary: The blocker is a request-time precondition. Race prevention between blocker check and archive is decided by WF-PRE-11.
forbidden: Workforce reads groups or group_teacher_assignments tables
Workforce changes Group assignments while archiving
Academic Groups changes Teacher lifecycle state
seams.transition: Replace groupsCount/direct SQL with a Groups-owned semantic query after WF-PRE-09/10.

seams.direction: coordinator -> Lesson Delivery public blocker query

authority.lesson-delivery: Whether a non-cancelled tenant Lesson on/after the effective current date blocks Teacher archive
authority.workforce: Teacher archive decision after all external preconditions pass

seams.interaction: The provider returns a semantic blocker decision using its own Lesson lifecycle meaning and clock/date policy.
seams.failurePolicy: Unknown or unavailable blocker state fails closed; Teacher is not archived.
seams.consistencyBoundary: The blocker is a request-time precondition. Race prevention and effective clock ownership are finalized in WF-PRE-09/11.
forbidden: Workforce reads lessons, groups, or schedules to derive the blocker
Workforce changes Lesson lifecycle while archiving
Lesson Delivery changes Teacher lifecycle state
seams.transition: Replace upcomingLessons raw projection use with a Lesson-owned blocker query after WF-PRE-09/10.

seams.capability: Teacher directory and profile compatibility composition
seams.direction: coordinator -> provider public read contracts; no provider -> coordinator dependency

authority.providers: Each context remains authoritative for its facts
authority.coordinator: Owns only compatibility composition, ordering/limit mapping, authorization-safe selection, and final HTTP DTO mapping

seams.interaction: The coordinator first obtains an actor-safe Workforce Teacher reference/projection, then requests only approved provider facts. It produces a non-authoritative compatibility read model.
seams.failurePolicy: A required provider failure fails the whole query; no partial profile, stale fallback, raw database row, or cross-tenant result is returned.
seams.consistencyBoundary: No distributed snapshot is required for this read. Each provider result is request-time authoritative; version skew is tolerated only within the frozen observable contract and must be characterized by WF-PRE-13.
forbidden: Workforce Domain/Application imports Group, Scheduling, Lesson, Student, or Identity internals
The coordinator becomes an authoritative data store
Provider credentials, finance fields, or mutable entities cross the seam
Teacher self profile exposes Group monthlyFee in the approved target
seams.transition: WF-PRE-09 defines exact provider/result DTOs; WF-PRE-10 defines focused query ports/adapters; WFT-PROFILE-006/007 distinguish legacy privacy characterization from approved target remediation.

seams.direction: coordinator -> Audit & History public append command

authority.workforce: Business action and affected Workforce reference
authority.audit-history: Audit record identity, immutable storage, actor/tenant conventions, retention, and query

seams.interaction: After a successful business outcome, the coordinator submits minimum audit intent containing verified actor/tenant, action, entity type/reference, and correlation metadata.
seams.failurePolicy: The compatibility workflow does not acknowledge success when mandatory audit append fails. It must not fabricate or locally persist an Audit-owned record.
seams.consistencyBoundary: Audit is not written inside a Workforce transaction. Commit ordering, durable handoff, retry, compensation, and reconciliation are blocking WF-PRE-11/12 decisions.
forbidden: Workforce writes audit_logs or platform_audit_logs
Audit & History writes Workforce state
Sensitive credential material is included in audit intent
seams.transition: Use synchronous append for first-extraction contract design unless WF-PRE-12 approves a versioned reliable event.

operationSeamDisposition.reason: Teacher and Working Hour facts are both Workforce-owned; verified actor/tenant context is a platform interface precondition, not a Workforce business seam.

blockingRisks.issue: Current Working Hour creation accepts explicit branchId without validation.
blockingRisks.treatment: Do not reproduce direct Branch-table access or silently bless invalid references. WF-PRE-08/09/13 must approve the exact compatibility/remediation treatment before target routing.

blockingRisks.issue: Current Teacher self-profile can expose Group monthlyFee.
blockingRisks.treatment: Target composition omits monthlyFee and records the intentional compatibility delta; security parity test WFT-PROFILE-007 remains mandatory.

blockingRisks.issue: Current Teacher/Identity writes share one SQLite transaction.
blockingRisks.treatment: No extraction of create/update/archive before WF-PRE-11 approves ordering, compensation, retry, and reconciliation.

blockingRisks.issue: Current audit append occurs after business persistence and can return failure after state committed.
blockingRisks.treatment: WF-PRE-11/12 must approve durable handoff and response semantics; no local cross-context audit write is permitted.

deferredDecisions.WF-PRE-08: Exact table ownership/access manifest and any temporary legacy adapter exception.
deferredDecisions.WF-PRE-09: Exact public commands, queries, DTOs, semantic errors, authorization context, and Teacher reference/status contract.
deferredDecisions.WF-PRE-10: Exact focused port boundaries and adapter ownership.
deferredDecisions.WF-PRE-11: Transaction ordering, atomicity, compensation, retry, idempotency, reconciliation, and authority transition.
deferredDecisions.WF-PRE-12: Whether any approved seam is replaced or supplemented by a versioned integration event.
deferredDecisions.WF-PRE-13: Contract, failure, tenant, parity, privacy, and rollback test implementation.
deferredDecisions.WF-PRE-14: Per-operation migration routing and rollback execution.
